{{External|rs|rsc}}
{{Has quick guide}}
{{Otheruses|the quest|the shield itself|Shield of arrav (item)|the book about the shield|Book (Shield of Arrav)}}
{{Infobox Quest
|name = Shield of Arrav
|number = 6
|image = [[File:Shield of Arrav.png|300px]]
|release = [[4 January]] [[2001]]
|update = Runescape beta is now online!
|members = No
|series = None
|developer = [[Paul Gower]]
}}
'''Shield of Arrav''' is a quest where the shield of the famous [[Arrav]] of [[Avarrocka]] has been stolen by organised crime syndicates in Varrock.

==Details==
{{Quest details
|start = [[Varrock]] Palace library. Talk to [[Reldo]], the librarian, located at the northern end of [[Varrock Castle]].
|startmap = 3209,3495
|difficulty = Novice
|description = Varrockian literature tells of a valuable shield, stolen long ago from the museum of [[Varrock]], by a gang of professional thieves. See if you can track down this shield and return it to the museum. You will need a friend to help you complete this quest.
|length = Short
|requirements = * A trustworthy friend or partner. Players can join the official [[chat-channel]] "OSRS SOA" to look for a partner.
|items = *Phoenix Gang: 20 [[coins]]
*Black Arm Gang: 2 [[Phoenix crossbow]]s (obtained during the quest)
|recommended = 
* {{SCP|Combat|10|link=yes}}
|kills = *Black Arm Gang: [[Weaponsmaster]] ''(level 23)''
*Phoenix Gang: [[Jonny the Beard]] ''(level 2)''
|leagueRegion = <poem>
Unlocking {{LeagueRegion|Asgarnia}} {{okay}} will auto-complete the quest
Unlocking {{LeagueRegion|Fremennik}} {{okay}} will auto-complete the quest
Unlocking {{LeagueRegion|Kandarin}} {{okay}} will auto-complete the quest
</poem>
}}

==Walkthrough==
===Choosing your gang===
[[File:Reldo chathead.png|left]]

Talk to [[Reldo]] in the [[Varrock Palace library]], the northernmost room on the ground floor. Ask him for a quest, and he will suggest you look around for a book called the Shield of Arrav, which may have a quest for you. To find it, search the [[bookcase (Shield of Arrav)|bookcase]] with a blue and white book in the top shelf, located on the east side of the short bookcases in the middle of the room. Read the book, then speak with Reldo again to tell him you have read it. The player will ask him about the whereabouts of the [[Phoenix Gang]] and the [[Black Arm Gang]]. At this stage, you can choose to join either gang. If you wish to join the Phoenix Gang, speak to Baraek, the fur trader in the market. Otherwise, talk to [[Charlie the Tramp]] next to the southern gate of Varrock.

'''Note''': You will only need to follow the steps to join one of the two gangs. It is recommended to '''make sure you have a partner from the opposite gang''' before choosing one. To find one, you may ask in the official [[chat-channel]] ''OSRS SOA''.

{{Colour|red|Warning:}} Although the majority of players in the chat-channel offer their services for free, some players also charge [[coins|in-game money]]. Beware of the possibility of being [[scams|scammed]] when engaging in [[scams#Trust trading scams|trust trades]] with other players.

When picking your gang, there are a number of considerations:
*It is much easier to find Phoenix Gang members via the official chat-channel.
*Phoenix Gang is recommended for lower combat levels.
*Black Arm Gang is recommended if you do not want to gain combat experience.
*If your partner has previously combined the two half pieces to create the complete certificate or has completed the quest, they cannot obtain the half piece of the shield from their gang hideout - which means certificates cannot be obtained from them. If your partner is from Phoenix Gang, they can help you acquire the key to the weapon store only.
*The half shield of arrav reappears in their respective locations if the half certificate is lost, allowing players to repeatedly offer half certificates as long as they have not completed the quest.

<choose>
<option>
{{:Shield of Arrav/Phoenix Gang}}
{{:Shield of Arrav/Black Arm Gang}}</option>
<option>
{{:Shield of Arrav/Black Arm Gang}}
{{:Shield of Arrav/Phoenix Gang}}</option>
</choose>

===Finishing up===
{{Needed|[[Broken shield]]}}

Take your half of the shield to [[Curator Haig Halen]], located in the [[Varrock Museum]]. He will take your half of the shield and give you two copies of [[Half certificate|half the certificate]]. Your partner, who has the other half of the Shield, will receive the other half of the certificate. Trade one half of the certificate with your partner for the other half. When you have both certificate halves, use them on the other to obtain a completed [[Certificate (Shield of Arrav)|certificate]]. If completing the quest as an [[Ironman]] you must 'use' the key/certificate on each other. [[File:Shield of Arrav - Certificate.png|centre]]

Take the certificate to [[King Roald]] in [[Varrock Palace]] to receive your reward. Congratulations, quest complete!

==Rewards==
{{Quest rewards
|name = Shield of Arrav
|image = [[File:Shield of Arrav reward scroll.png|centre]]
|qp = 1
|rewards =
*600 [[coins]]
*Ability to claim 5 [[Kudos]] and an [[Antique lamp (Varrock Museum)#Shield of Arrav|Antique lamp]] rewarding 1,000 XP in any skill of at least level 20 from [[Historian Minas]] after completion of the quest (members only)
}}

==Required for completing==
Completion of Shield of Arrav is required for the following:
* [[Heroes' Quest]]
* [[Defender of Varrock]]
* [[Ethically Acquired Antiquities]]

==Transcript==
{{Hastranscript|quest}}

==Notes==
*If a player cannot find someone to trade the certificates with, they cannot finish the quest. 
*Join the osrs soa  friend chat.
*A Black Arm Gang member can enter the Phoenix Gang Weapon Storage with a key from a Phoenix Gang member.
*Phoenix crossbows can be obtained by Phoenix gang members either by going into the weapon store and telekinetic grabbing them, or by killing the [[weaponsmaster]] and picking up the two crossbows. However, the crossbows must be traded to a Black Arm gang member in order to gain trust to Katrine.
*If collecting crossbows, they can respawn three times before the [[weaponsmaster]] does.
*Additional certificate halves can be obtained by speaking to the curator provided the player has turned in a shield half prior and has not finished the quest nor in possession of a full certificate. The halves received will be the same as the one's received before. There is no practical limit to how many certificate halves you can have. '''Note: Once you finish the quest, or are in possession of a full certificate, you can no longer get spare shields or speak to the curator for additional certificate halves.''' However, you can continue to keep certificate halves in your bank account indefinitely after you have completed the quest or trade for them with other players.
*New player trade restrictions apply to quest items, but it is still possible to trade the items by using them on the other player.
*The player who joined the Phoenix Gang will receive 2 left halves, and the player who joined the Black Arm Gang will receive 2 right halves.
*You can get multiple certificate halves by talking to [[Curator Haig Halen]].

==Changes==
{{Subject changes header}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 6 November 2024
|update = The Curse of Arrav & Mobile Anniversary Update
|change = Changes were made to the quest with the release of [[The Curse of Arrav]], including but not limited to: 
* Updated dialogue
* Jonny the Beard is now only attackable when tasked by Straven with doing so, and permanently disappears after the quest for those who killed him.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 13 July 2017
|update = Lizardman Shaman Improvements
|change = Corrected some spelling in the Shield of Arrav quest.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 29 May 2007
|update = Varrock's New Look
|change = The quest was updated with the redesign of Varrock. Before this update, to finish the quest, one of the players would need to give their partner their half of the shield. The person with both halves would then hand them in to the Curator, and get two certificates in return. They would then have to give one of the two certificates to the partner to finish the quest.<br/>
After this update, a system that mitigates any element of trust was implemented, where shield halves can be handed in for certificate halves, which can then be exchanged in a single trade.
}}
{{Subject changes footer}}

==Trivia==
*Shield of Arrav was one of the 6 quests available during ''RuneScape Classic''{{'}}s launch.
*Once a player finishes the quest, they can no longer take any halves of the shield from their storage places. However, players can still acquire the halves of the shield from other players (via trading, drops, or general stores).
*In the beginning, a bug allowed the player to join both gangs. Katrine would appear angry at the player, but the player would still be able to enter the Black Arm Gang. The Phoenix gang doorman would also attack the player.
*In the early days, entering the Phoenix Gang's headquarters required a separate key. This was later changed, and the key was removed from the game.
*The shield is currently on display (display number 24) at the [[Varrock museum|Varrock Museum]] after you speak to the curator on the second floor and tell him how you recovered the shield for the museum.
*Before the shield is put onto display in the [[Varrock museum|Varrock Museum]], it mentions "King Roald has offered a 1,200 coins bounty for the reward of the shield and we keep this display case empty in the hopes it will one day be returned." However, King Roald rewards the adventurer only 600 coins for quest completion - half of the advertised offer from the display case. This is because the bounty was split with the other player.
*Although it's possible to start the quest with a complete certificate, attempting to turn it in to King Roald yields him saying, "The name on this certificate isn't yours! I can't give you the reward unless you do the quest yourself!"

{{Shield of Arrav}}
